verses.text: The LORD in the wilderness of Sinai, the first day of the second month, the second year after Egypt: take the sum of Israel by families and fathers' houses, every male from twenty upward, all able to go to war. Guidance: One year after the Passover, one month after the tabernacle stood up, God numbers His people. The book is Numbers in English, but in Hebrew In the Wilderness. The count is for the journey, not for the trophy case.

The LORD in the wilderness of Sinai, the first day of the second month, the second year after Egypt: take the sum of Israel by families and fathers' houses, every male from twenty upward, all able to go to war. Guidance: One year after the Passover, one month after the tabernacle stood up, God numbers His people. The book is Numbers in English, but in Hebrew In the Wilderness. The count is for the journey, not for the trophy case.

The LORD in the wilderness of Sinai, the first day of the second month, the second year after Egypt: take the sum of Israel by families and fathers' houses, every male from twenty upward, all able to go to war. Guidance: One year after the Passover, one month after the tabernacle stood up, God numbers His people. The book is Numbers in English, but in Hebrew In the Wilderness. The count is for the journey, not for the trophy case.

A man of every tribe — head of his fathers' house. Reuben: Elizur. Simeon: Shelumiel. Judah: Nahshon. Issachar: Nethaneel. Zebulun: Eliab. Ephraim: Elishama. Manasseh: Gamaliel. Benjamin: Abidan. Dan: Ahiezer. Asher: Pagiel. Gad: Eliasaph. Naphtali: Ahira. Princes of fathers' tribes. Guidance: Twelve names you'd skip; God doesn't. Every tribe gets a leader on the day the camp is counted. The names are written so no family is forgotten. God knows each by birth.

Moses and Aaron assembled the congregation by families. Reuben 46,500. Simeon 59,300. Gad 45,650. Judah 74,600. Issachar 54,400. Zebulun 57,400. Ephraim 40,500. Manasseh 32,200. Benjamin 35,400. Dan 62,700. Asher 41,500. Naphtali 53,400. Total: 603,550. Guidance: Six hundred thousand fighting men — before women, children, old men. From seventy who went into Egypt to this. God's promise to Abraham is no longer abstract; it has a head count.

Levites were not numbered. The LORD: number not Levi. Appoint Levites over the tabernacle — they bear, minister, encamp round it. When it sets forward they take it down; when it pitches, set it up. Stranger that comes nigh — put to death. Levites encamp round the tabernacle, that no wrath be on the congregation. Guidance: Levi has a different census — for service, not war. They camp closest to God; their work is His house. Their nearness protects Israel from God's holy fire.
